user_message: Working on Fourth Coffee's territory planning. Got the escalation email -- help me identify the blockers.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 15:32 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-28 12:17 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 12:26 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fourth Coffee: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-D trap (Response draft over-eager / 'where have you been' tone); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-D failure mode (Response draft over-eager / 'where have you been' tone)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-D -- Response draft over-eager / 'where have you been' tone
